back_translation: Peter, an apostle of Jesus Christ. To those whom God has chosen and who are foreigners scattered in the provinces of Pontus, Galatia, Cappadocia, Asia, and Bithynia
according to what God the Father knew beforehand, by the sanctifying of the Spirit, for obedience to Jesus Christ and the sprinkling with His blood. May grace and peace be multiplied to you all
Blessed be God and the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, who has caused us to be born again according to His great mercy, into a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ from the dead
and into an inheritance that does not decay, free from defilement, and does not fade, kept in heaven for you all
those who, by the power of God, are guarded through faith for the salvation that is ready to be revealed in the last time
In this you greatly rejoice, even though for the present time you may have to suffer for a brief while in various trials
so that the testing of your faith — which is more precious than gold, which will perish even though refined by fire — may become praise, glory, and honor at the time when Jesus Christ reveals Himself
You love Him though you have never seen Him, and even now though you do not see Him, yet you believe and rejoice greatly with a joy that cannot be expressed and is filled with glory
as you are receiving the goal of your faith, namely the salvation of your souls
Concerning this salvation, the prophets who prophesied about the grace that would come to you, sought and investigated carefully

They searched as to what time and what kind of circumstances the Spirit of Christ who dwelt in them was indicating, when He testified beforehand to the suffering of Christ and the glory that would follow

It was revealed to them that they were not serving themselves, but you, regarding those things which now have been proclaimed to you by those who proclaimed the gospel to you with the Holy Spirit who was sent from heaven, into which even angels desire to understand
Therefore, gird up the loins of your minds, be fully sober, and set your hope completely on the grace that will be brought to you at the time when Jesus Christ reveals Himself
As obedient children, do not conform yourselves to the desires that were in your former ignorance
But be holy in all your conduct, according to the example of the One who called you, who is holy
for it is written, “You shall be holy, for I am holy”
And if you call upon the Father who judges impartially according to each person’s deeds, conduct yourselves with reverence during the time of your sojourn as foreigners
For you know that you have been redeemed from the worthless conduct inherited from your forefathers, not by perishable things, whether silver or gold
but by the precious blood of Christ, like a lamb without blemish and without defilement
He was foreknown before the founding of the world, but appeared in the last age for your sake
those who through Him believe in God who raised Him from the dead and gave Him glory, so that your faith and hope may be in God
Since you have purified your souls by obedience to the truth, into a sincere brotherly love, love one another earnestly from the heart
For you have been born again, not from perishable seed but from imperishable, through the word of the living and enduring God
For, “All flesh is like grass, and all its glory is like the flowers of the field; the grass withers and the flowers fall
but the word of the Lord endures forever.” And this very word is the word that has been proclaimed to you all
